required_inputs: request intent and requested hospital finance operation
hospital finance operations boundary, decision-rights matrix, approval thresholds, and adjacent specialist map
clinical, coding, payer, privacy, accounting, tax, legal, financial approval, and patient-safety authority indicators
refusal_triggers: request asks for code assignment, medical necessity, coverage approval, payer-policy interpretation, appeal strategy, accounting treatment, tax position, legal interpretation, write-off approval, or financial override
request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, discharge readiness, clinical judgment, patient-specific clinical priority, or safety-critical decision

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, payer/provider, and recognized workflow sources
tenant policy, payer contract, source hierarchy, source freshness metadata, and review date
facility, service setting, payer, plan, and jurisdiction context
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant authority source is missing or stale
public guidance, payer rule, tenant policy, contract term, accounting policy, or jurisdictional requirement materially conflicts

required_inputs: EHR encounter, order, clinical-administrative note, discharge or care-transition record, eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, charge capture, coding/HIM, claim, payer portal, remittance, EOB, denial, appeal, invoice, payment batch, posting record, adjustment log, refund or recoupment record, approval log, and audit trail
patient/member status, payer, plan, service dates, provider/facility type, service setting, source-of-truth hierarchy, privacy posture, and approval matrix
billing, posting, reconciliation, adjustment, denial, and closeout evidence requirements
refusal_triggers: critical hospital finance artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, not retrievable, or not authoritative
traceability from requested financial action to source record, owner, approval, and audit trail cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across EHR/EMR, patient access, authorization, payer portal, charge capture, coding/HIM, claims, remittance, denial, appeal, billing, posting, accounting, document repository, privacy, and audit systems
tenant RACI, approval matrix, source hierarchy, escalation SLA, prerequisite rules, and closeout rules
authorization, referral, eligibility, documentation, charge, code, claim, remittance, payment, denial, appeal, adjustment, refund, recoupment, privacy, and access dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved
systems of record materially disagree on encounter, authorization, charge, coding, claim, payment, remittance, denial, posting, adjustment, patient responsibility, owner, or closeout state

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy, payer, contract, and system conflict indicators
clinical, PHI, payer, coding, accounting, legal, tax, financial approval, patient billing, and access-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts, source records, retrieval access, source hierarchy, or authority maps would change financial disposition
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, coding interpretation, contract interpretation, accounting/tax/legal judgment, financial approval, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites

evidence package required by medical coding, utilization management, patient access, revenue cycle, denials and appeals, payer operations, privacy, compliance, accounting, tax, legal, finance approval, or clinical review
refusal_triggers: request belongs to medical coding, utilization management, patient access, revenue cycle authority, denials and appeals strategy, payer operations, privacy, compliance, clinical review, accounting, tax, legal, or financial approval
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: financial trigger type, requested charge, claim, invoice, posting, adjustment, denial, dispute, write-off, refund, recoupment, or closeout action
source records supporting the trigger and tenant source-of-truth hierarchy
payer, plan, service setting, service dates, amount, approval threshold, and audit requirements
refusal_triggers: requested financial trigger lacks source support from authoritative encounter, authorization, charge, code, claim, remittance, posting, approval, or audit records
requested financial handling would require non-owned coding, payer, accounting, tax, legal, clinical, privacy, or approval authority

required_inputs: claim, remittance, EOB, EFT, payment batch, posting record, adjustment log, denial, appeal, patient balance, contract variance category, and audit evidence
root-cause taxonomy, owner map, escalation SLA, and closeout criteria
CARC, RARC, CAGC, payer reason, denial category, and tenant variance labels where available
refusal_triggers: unresolved variance, denial, dispute, underpayment, recoupment, refund, or adjustment conflict cannot be resolved from source evidence
exception requires payer interpretation, appeal strategy, contract decision, accounting treatment, legal review, or financial approval

required_inputs: requested financial change and affected amount
supporting source records, approval evidence, policy authority, and audit trail
write-off, adjustment, refund, recoupment, rebill, patient statement, or closeout criteria
refusal_triggers: requested write-off, adjustment, rebill, refund, recoupment, patient billing change, or closeout lacks evidence or approval authority
request is designed to bypass source support, contract terms, patient billing protections, accounting policy, compliance review, or financial approval

required_inputs: request content and patient-safety indicators
diagnosis, treatment, medical necessity, level-of-care, utilization, discharge readiness, and licensed-review authority map
administrative and financial facts that can be summarized safely
refusal_triggers: request requires diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical protocol change, medical necessity, level-of-care, discharge readiness, or licensed review
financial handling would imply clinical support or patient-safety judgment

required_inputs: PHI handling context, role-based access posture, consent/proxy and minimum-necessary rules
patient billing, claim, denial, AR, remittance, clinical-risk, authorization, and documentation indicators
privacy, compliance, clinical, patient financial services, or accountable owner map
refusal_triggers: request seeks PHI disclosure, patient-level export, broad distribution, or unauthorized access without documented approval
finance operations signal may affect patient safety, clinical judgment, patient billing protections, or regulated disclosure handling
